root_cause: Derailment — track failure
narrative_text: Ranchi Express derailed approximately 200 km from Kolkata near Ranchi, Jharkhand. Track failure cited as primary cause. 47 deaths.

root_cause: Collision — probable signalling human error
narrative_text: Punjab Mail collided with a freight train at Dumraon, Bihar. 100+ deaths. Signalling human error strongly suspected in CRS inquiry.

root_cause: Derailment — cause undetermined
narrative_text: Udyan Abha Toofan Express derailed near Etmadpur, close to Tundla Junction, Uttar Pradesh. 100+ deaths.

root_cause: Rameswaram Cyclone storm surge washed away train
narrative_text: The Pamban–Dhanushkodi Passenger train was washed off the track by a massive cyclone storm surge near Dhanushkodi, Tamil Nadu. 200+ deaths. The town itself was destroyed and abandoned after this incident.

root_cause: Points setting error — human error
narrative_text: A freight train collided with a stationary passenger train near Jaipur, Rajasthan. 85 deaths, 130 injured. CRS attributed the collision to incorrect points setting — a direct human error by station staff.

root_cause: Derailment into Magari River
narrative_text: An express train derailed and plunged into the Magari River near Muhammadabad, Ghazipur district, UP. 70 deaths, 130 injured.

root_cause: Derailment on Bagmati River bridge during cyclone — 7 coaches into river
narrative_text: The Mansi–Saharsa Passenger train (overcrowded local) derailed while crossing the Bagmati River bridge near Mansi Junction, Khagaria, Bihar. Seven coaches plunged into the flooded river during cyclone conditions. Official death toll: 235; estimates range from 500 to 800+. Many bodies never recovered. India's deadliest rail accident. INDIA'S DEADLIEST RAILWAY DISASTER.

root_cause: Head-on collision
narrative_text: Narmada Express collided head-on with a freight train between Khdori and Bhanwar Tonk, Madhya Pradesh. 50 deaths, 49 injured.

root_cause: Derailment off bridge into Ashtamudi Lake — track/wheel maintenance failure
narrative_text: The Island Express (Bengaluru–Thiruvananthapuram) derailed and fell off the Peruman bridge into Ashtamudi Lake near Kollam, Kerala. 10 coaches sank. 106 deaths. CRS investigation found track and wheel maintenance issues were likely contributing factors.

root_cause: Rear-end collision — Purushottam Express hit stationary Kalindi Express. Signal failure + communication breakdown (human error)
narrative_text: Kalindi Express stopped after hitting a nilgai on the tracks near Firozabad. Purushottam Express ploughed into the stationary train at full speed. Signal system failed to alert following trains. Communication breakdown between station master and crew. 358 deaths — 2nd deadliest in independent India. CRS: moisture ingress destroyed signal electronics, staff notification system non-functional.

root_cause: Track fracture derailment + fog collision — inspection overdue (human error)
narrative_text: Golden Temple Mail derailed due to fractured track near Khanna, Punjab. Sealdah Express then collided with derailed coaches in thick fog. Track maintenance interval had been exceeded by 4 days. No speed restriction posted. 212 deaths. CRS: track inspection overdue due to resource shortage — human/administrative failure.

root_cause: Head-on collision — signalling error during maintenance. Both trains cleared into single line (human error)
narrative_text: Avadh Assam Express and Brahmaputra Mail collided head-on at Gaisal station, West Bengal. Signal entered ERROR state during maintenance window. Station master and signal tower staff communicated incorrectly, clearing both trains into the same single line. 285 deaths. CRS finding: classic signalling human error compounded by goods train crew unfamiliar with diversion route.

root_cause: Maoist/Naxalite sabotage — fish plates removed from bridge track
narrative_text: Howrah Rajdhani Express derailed and plunged into the Dhave River near Rafiganj, Gaya district, Bihar. Maoist (Naxalite) militants had removed fish plates from the bridge track. 130–150 deaths, 150+ injured. CRS confirmed deliberate sabotage.

root_cause: Musi River bridge washed away by flash flood
narrative_text: Delta Fast Passenger train derailed when the Musi River bridge near Valigonda, Nalgonda district, Andhra Pradesh was washed away by flash floods. 114 deaths, 200+ injured.

root_cause: Maoist sabotage — track removed. Secondary collision by goods train
narrative_text: Jnaneswari Express derailed near Jhargram, West Bengal, after Maoist militants removed a section of track. A goods train then hit the derailed coaches. 146–148 deaths, 200+ injured.

root_cause: Derailment — 15 coaches. Track maintenance lapse (human error)
narrative_text: Howrah–Kalka Mail derailed near Fatehpur/Malwan, Uttar Pradesh. 15 coaches went off the tracks. CRS inquiry found track maintenance lapse. 68–70 deaths, 239–300+ injured.

root_cause: Signalling or track maintenance error (human error)
narrative_text: Dehradun–Varanasi Janata Express derailed near Batan railway station, Uttar Pradesh. 58 deaths, 150+ injured. Signalling or track maintenance human error cited in reports.

root_cause: Fractured rail — track inspection lapse (human error). Overcrowded train.
narrative_text: Indore–Patna Express derailed near Pukhrayan, Kanpur Dehat, Uttar Pradesh. 14 coaches derailed. Fractured rail was primary cause; track inspection had lapsed. Train was heavily overcrowded. 146–152 deaths, 200+ injured.

root_cause: Electronic interlocking error — maintenance reconfiguration not updated in signal system (human error)
narrative_text: Triple collision near Bahanaga Bazar, Balasore, Odisha. Coromandel Express (12841) hit a goods train on the loop line then the Yesvantpur–Howrah Express hit the wreckage. Signal showed GREEN but loop line was incorrectly routed as main line due to incomplete maintenance reconfiguration. 296 deaths, 900–1,200+ injured. Worst accident in decades. Preliminary probe confirmed human/technical interference in electronic signalling system.

root_cause: Goods train crew failed to observe RED signal (human error). Brake failure suspected.
narrative_text: Kanchanjungha Express (13015) was rear-ended by a heavy goods train near New Jalpaiguri on the Siliguri–Kolkata main line. Goods train crew failed to observe the RED signal. Heavy consist of 92 containers reduced braking efficiency. 15 deaths, 37 injured. CRS: crew human error — failure to observe danger signal.

root_cause: Signal display fault (moisture-affected) + over-speeding on delayed recovery schedule
narrative_text: Passenger train 14101 collided with a goods train at Vizianagaram. Signal technicians found the signal head had accumulated moisture showing YELLOW instead of RED. Driver responded appropriately to received signal but over-speeding on delayed schedule contributed to severity. Signal device had not been serviced in 8 months. 14 deaths, 23 injured.
